# Contributor License Agreement (CLA)

Thanks for contributing to Flow Translate! Before we can merge your code, we
need you to agree to this Contributor License Agreement. It protects you, the
project, and every user.

**Plain-language summary** (the legal terms below are what actually count):

- You keep ownership of your contribution.
- You give Tom Huang ("the owner") a broad license to use your contribution,
  **including the right to release it under other licenses** — for example, a
  future commercial or closed-source version of Flow Translate.
- You confirm you actually wrote it and have the right to contribute it.

This is what lets the project stay open **and** lets the owner build a product
on top of it. It is adapted from the widely used Apache Individual CLA.

By submitting a pull request and signing (see
[CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)), you agree to the following:

---

## 1. Definitions

"You" means the individual or legal entity making a Contribution.

"Contribution" means any original work of authorship — code, documentation,
or other material — that you intentionally submit to the project (for example,
via a pull request or patch).

"The owner" means Tom Huang, the maintainer and copyright holder of Flow
Translate.

## 2. You keep your copyright

You retain all right, title, and interest in your Contribution. This agreement
does **not** transfer ownership.

## 3. Copyright license you grant

You grant the owner and all recipients of software distributed by the owner a
perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free, irrevocable
copyright license to reproduce, prepare derivative works of, publicly display,
publicly perform, sublicense, and distribute your Contribution and derivative
works.

**Re-licensing right.** You expressly agree that the owner may license and
distribute your Contribution (and works based on it) **under any license terms,
including proprietary/commercial and closed-source terms**, in addition to
Apache-2.0. This lets the owner offer commercial versions of Flow Translate.

## 4. Patent license you grant

You grant the owner and recipients a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive,
no-charge, royalty-free, irrevocable (except as stated below) patent license to
make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, import, and otherwise transfer your
Contribution, for patent claims you can license that are necessarily infringed
by your Contribution alone or combined with the project. If anyone starts patent
litigation claiming the Contribution infringes, patent licenses granted for that
Contribution end for them.

## 5. You have the right to contribute

You represent that:

- Each Contribution is your original creation, or you have the rights to submit
  it; and
- If your employer has rights to work you create, you have permission to make
  the Contribution on their behalf, or your employer has waived those rights.

## 6. Third-party material

If your Contribution includes work you did not write, you will clearly identify
it and its license/source when you submit it.

## 7. No warranty / no obligation

You provide your Contribution "AS IS", without warranties. The owner is not
obligated to use or merge any Contribution.

---

### How to sign

You do **not** sign this file directly. When you open your first pull request,
an automated CLA check ([`workflows/cla.yml`](workflows/cla.yml))
will ask you to agree by posting a one-line comment. Your agreement is recorded
against your GitHub username for future pull requests.

If automation isn't set up yet, add this line to your pull request description
and we'll record it manually:

> I have read the CLA and I agree to it. My GitHub username is @your-username.
